name: Denmark
topIncomeNote: Includes the 8% labour-market tax and the new 5% top-top bracket on income above DKK 2,818,152 introduced in 2026; excludes church tax (avg. ~0.64%, optional). Verified against PwC Worldwide Tax Summaries.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; highest in the OECD.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Finland
topIncomeNote: National top rate of 37.5% on income above EUR 52,100 plus average municipal tax of 7.5% (range 4.7-10.9%) for 2026, per PwC. Excludes church tax (1-2.25%) and the capped public-broadcasting tax. Down sharply from 51.8% in 2025 (Tax Foundation).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Iceland
topIncomeNote: National top bracket of 31.35% plus the maximum/average municipal rate of 14.94% = 46.29% for 2026 (PwC). Municipal rates vary from 12.44% to 14.94%.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Norway
topIncomeNote: 22% flat tax on general income plus the top bracket-tax rate of 17.8% on personal income above NOK 1,467,200 for 2026 (PwC); excludes social security contributions. Tax Foundation's 2026 table shows 39.6% (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/); the PwC statutory components are used here.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; fell 1.4 percentage points from 2023, the largest decline among European OECD countries.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Sweden
topIncomeNote: 20% national tax on taxable income above SEK 643,000 plus an average municipal rate of about 32% (PwC 2026). Combined 52.3% per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/), which uses the precise average municipal rate.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Austria
topIncomeNote: The 55% top rate applies only to income above EUR 1,000,000; the next bracket (EUR 104,859-1,000,000) is taxed at 50% (PwC 2026).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; third-highest in the OECD.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Belgium
topIncomeNote: 50% federal top rate plus an average communal surcharge of 7% of the income tax due (communal rates range 0-9%), per PwC (income year 2025); matches Tax Foundation's 2026 combined figure.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: France
topIncomeNote: Combined figure per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/). The statutory top bracket is 45%, plus the exceptional contribution on high incomes (3-4% above EUR 250,000/500,000) and CSG/CRDS-type charges; from 2025 a differential contribution (CDHR) enforces a 20% minimum tax on high incomes (PwC).
corporateNote: Includes surcharges. A temporary exceptional contribution raises the rate to 36.1% for the first fiscal year ending on or after 31 December 2025 (Tax Foundation).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; second-highest in the OECD.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Germany
topIncomeNote: 45% top rate (taxable income above EUR 277,826 single) plus the 5.5% solidarity surcharge levied on the tax = 47.5% (PwC; Tax Foundation 2026). Church tax (8-9% of the income tax, for church members) excluded.
corporateNote: 15% federal CIT plus 5.5% solidarity surcharge (15.825%) plus municipal trade tax varying roughly 8.75-20.3%; overall burden is ~30% in Berlin and ~33% in Munich (PwC). Federal CIT is legislated to fall stepwise from 2028. 30.1% is Tax Foundation's 2026 combined figure.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Ireland
topIncomeNote: Income tax top rate is 40% (on income above EUR 44,000 single, PwC 2026); the 48.0% combined figure per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/) additionally reflects surtaxes (the Universal Social Charge).
corporateNote: Lowest headline corporate rate among these countries, per Tax Foundation's 2026 table.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; the ratio fell from 27.7% in 2010, the largest decline in the OECD over that period.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Luxembourg
topIncomeNote: 42% top bracket (income above EUR 234,870) plus the 9% employment-fund solidarity surcharge on the tax for high earners = 45.78% (PwC 2025; unchanged in Tax Foundation's 2026 table).
corporateNote: 16% CIT plus 7% solidarity surtax plus 6.75% Luxembourg City municipal business tax = 23.87% effective combined rate for Luxembourg City (PwC 2025).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).
taxToGdpNote: 2024 provisional figure, OECD Revenue Statistics 2025; rose 1.7 percentage points from 2023, among the largest OECD increases.

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Netherlands
topIncomeNote: 49.5% on Box 1 (employment and home-ownership) income above EUR 78,426 (PwC 2026).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: Switzerland
topIncomeNote: Combined figure for a typical canton per Tax Foundation's 2026 table (https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/top-personal-income-tax-rates-europe/). The federal top rate is 11.5%; cantonal and communal taxes vary widely by canton and commune (PwC), so the actual top rate depends on residence.
corporateNote: Federal CIT is 8.5% on profit after tax (~7.8% pre-tax); combined federal + cantonal + communal maximum rates range from about 11.7% to 20.5% depending on canton (PwC). 19.6% is Tax Foundation's 2026 representative combined figure.
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)

name: United Kingdom
topIncomeNote: Additional rate of 45% on income over GBP 125,140 for 2026/27 in England, Wales and Northern Ireland; Scotland's top rate on employment income is 48% (PwC).
vatNote: Standard rate as of January 2026 (Tax Foundation, https://taxfoundation.org/data/all/eu/value-added-tax-vat-rates-europe/).

sources.label: Tax Foundation (Corporate Income Tax Rates in Europe, 2026)
